purpose: BLIND CONFIRMATORY v2. Tests the post-v1 engineering change (decode config: census dict -> single dense-sweep-validated constant 64/2) on a fresh device instance and a FRESH held-out shape set. The v1 confirmatory (RESULTS-gate2-confirmatory.md) failed C1/C3 and narrowed the claim; this registers what the new selector is predicted to deliver and tests exactly that. Nothing here re-adjudicates v1: its verdict stands.
frozen_commit: 54492e8 (grouped-nf4-gemm main). No code, config, harness, or reducer changes between this stamp and the reported results. Any deviation voids the run and must be re-registered.
provenance_of_bars: Every threshold below is derived from data that existed BEFORE this stamp: the two committed config sweeps (sweep_a5000.json / sweep_a2000.json, bench/phase2/decode_config_sweep.py) and the v1 confirmatory reps. The held-out-v2 shapes have NEVER been measured (only grid-NEIGHBOR predictions exist, listed below as falsifiable predictions). Two held-out-v2 (N,K) pairs coincide with grid points (granite gate_up 1024x1536, Qwen3-Next gate_up 1024x2048); the selector is a single constant chosen from 720-cell structure, not a per-cell fit, so pointwise leakage cannot select for them — disclosed anyway. Qwen3-Next top_k=10 exceeds the grid's T range (max 8): extrapolation, disclosed.

devices: FRESH RunPod SECURE A5000 (sm_86) provisioned AFTER this stamp; never used in tuning or sweeps
home RTX A2000 12GB (sm_86) — different SM count (26 vs 64), co-resident home-lab services (contention transients expected and tolerated per the bars)
protocol.cells: decode_bs1 x backends {dequant_grouped, gemv_4bit, fused_nf4, fused_nf4_v1cfg} x shapes: 8 census + 8 held-out-v2 (bench/phase1/heldout_v2_shapes.json: DeepSeek-V3, granite-3.1-3b-a800m, Llama-4-Scout-16E top_k=1, Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B top_k=10). fused_nf4_v1cfg = the SAME kernel forced onto the retired v1 census-dict config, so config claims are PAIRED (same process, same stack, same thermal context) — the v1 confirmatory showed unpaired cross-instance comparisons swing by up to 1.7x on the fused side while the dequant baseline is stable to a few %.
protocol.reps: n=3 independent harness invocations per device (fresh process each). Property suite once per device before timing.
protocol.energy: --energy-window 5.0; method + sample count recorded per cell.
protocol.reduction: bench/phase1/reduce_confirmatory_v2.py reading frozen_verdict_criteria_params from THIS file. Per cell: worst-rep and median-rep dequant_ms/fused_ms; worst-rep and median-rep v1cfg_ms/fused_ms (paired); worst-rep fused_J/dequant_J. Cells skipped on every rep of a device are NOT-RUN, excluded from that device's denominators one-for-one (DeepSeek-V3 E=256 stacks ~8-9 GB resident may OOM the 12 GB A2000; recorded, never substituted).

frozen_verdict_criteria.P1a_bounded_loss: PAIRED worst-rep fused/v1cfg time ratio (v1cfg_ms/fused_ms) >= 0.88 on ALL run cells, both devices. Meaning: the constant config never costs more than ~12% vs the retired per-shape dict anywhere, while eliminating its up-to-2.6x off-census default regret. Basis: min predicted paired = 0.913 (A2000 Qwen3-30B down) and 0.926 (A5000 OLMoE gate_up) from the sweeps, minus noise margin.
frozen_verdict_criteria.P1b_gains_exist: PAIRED median-rep ratio >= 1.10 on >= 2 run cells per device (any of the 16). Basis: sweeps predict >= 1.11 on 6 cells (A5000: Qwen3-30B gu 1.16, gemma gu 1.18, gemma dn 1.17, gpt-oss dn 1.18, granite gu 1.12, Qwen3-Next gu 1.11) and >= 1.11 on 3 cells (A2000: granite gu 1.17, Qwen3-Next gu 1.13, gpt-oss gu 1.12). Median-rep because gains-existence is a typical-case claim; bounded-loss P1a stays worst-rep.
frozen_verdict_criteria.S1_census_vs_dequant: Per device: median-rep ratio >= 1.3 on >= 7/8 census cells AND median-rep >= 1.0 on 8/8 AND worst-rep >= 1.0 on >= 7/8. Basis: v1 medians x predicted paired deltas put gpt-oss down (A5000) at ~1.2 and Qwen3-30B gu (A2000) at ~1.3 — hence 7/8 not 8/8; the worst-rep 7/8 floor tolerates exactly one home-card contention transient (v1 observed 1 in 48 cell-reps).
frozen_verdict_criteria.S2_heldout2_vs_dequant: Per device (NOT-RUN-scaled): median-rep >= 1.0 on >= 6/8 AND median-rep >= 1.3 on >= 4/8 AND worst-rep >= 0.9 on >= 6/8. Basis: launch-bound dequant baseline (~3 launches per active expert) predicts strong fused wins on the k=8/k=10 cells; Llama-4-Scout top_k=1 cells are REGISTERED AS AT-RISK (T=1 occupancy starvation, the Phi-3.5-down structural class from v1; split-K is the registered follow-on, not this change).
frozen_verdict_criteria.E1_energy: Per device: worst-rep fused J/token < dequant J/token on >= 15/16 run cells. v1 measured 32/32; the new T=1 cells could compress margins — one surprise per device is allowed and reported, not hidden.
frozen_verdict_criteria.no_tune_clause: Any failed criterion is reported at full volume and the claim narrowed accordingly; no re-runs until green, no reduction changes after data exists.

falsifiable_predictions_heldout2_paired.note: grid-NEIGHBOR predictions of v1cfg_ms/fused_ms (old default 128/4 vs 64/2), min..max over neighbor cells; A5000 then A2000
